notes.anchor: the eighth day before the Kalends of June
notes.note: Roman dates counted backward inclusively from the next named day; this is 25 May 58 BC.
The friction among the dynasts and the consuls of 58 (Pompey, Caesar, Piso, Gabinius); Cicero notes bitterly that they quarrel over everything except his recall.
Atticus had reproached Cicero for collapsing emotionally in exile; the charge of weakness (animo infirmo) recurs across these letters.
Quintus would have put on the squalor --- unkempt dress and beard --- of a mourner or suppliant on his brother's behalf; Cicero could not bear to see it.
Cicero hints that his ruin was contrived by intimates of his own household and circle, not only by open enemies.

notes.anchor: the fourteenth day before the Kalends of July
notes.note: Counting backward inclusively from 1 July, this is 18 June 58 BC.
